behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler↔signature-verifier↔queue handoff: reject path must both set HTTP 401 and suppress enqueue side effect. MOCK-MISS: this is cross-module behavior, not just one pure function. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required; in-process HTTP harness + fake queue can expose it. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate for higher levels: behavior text only requires '401' and 'not forwarded', not real network conditions or external APIs.
behaviors.test_description: Run the HTTP handler with a malformed `X-Signature-256` over a real request body, assert status 401, and assert queue `enqueue` was not called.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: Failure is ordering across modules (HTTP handler and queue persistence boundary). MOCK-MISS: unit-only logic tests miss sequencing between ack and write completion. REAL-INFRA: can be caught with in-process wiring using a controllable queue adapter (resolve write promise, then observe response). No LLM dependency. Rejection-gate for System: behavior specifies ordering, not real network/OS failure semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Use a queue test double that records write-start/write-finish timestamps and gates completion; verify HTTP 200 is not emitted until write-finish is observed.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: This is module orchestration: enqueue/dispatch must decouple from downstream worker completion. MOCK-MISS: pure unit tests won’t prove non-blocking handoff timing. REAL-INFRA: no real subprocess/network is required; delayed downstream promise in-process is sufficient. No AI/LLM steps. Rejection-gate for System: behavior text is about waiting semantics, not OS/network effects.
behaviors.test_description: Inject a downstream processor that blocks for a long duration; assert HTTP response is sent promptly while processor promise remains unresolved.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is runtime-level latency under concurrency and wall-clock SLA. REAL-INFRA: requires real timing behavior and concurrent request handling; in-process fakes/mocked timers can hide event-loop contention and server backpressure. MOCK-HIDE: mocked clock or isolated unit wiring may always pass while production server misses SLA. No LLM dependency. Rejection-gate for higher levels: no model decisions or multi-agent chain in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real service, create concurrent load with slow in-flight downstream work from earlier events, send a new valid webhook, and assert end-to-end HTTP latency stays <3s by wall clock.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at state handoff between dedupe logic and queue persistence (idempotency across requests). Unit tests of an ID-check function are insufficient to prove write suppression across module boundaries. REAL-INFRA: local in-process store/DB fixture is enough; no real network/OS needed. No LLM dependency. Rejection-gate for System: behavior does not require distributed/network dedupe semantics, only 'already received' not written again.
behaviors.test_description: Submit two webhook requests with the same event ID; assert first is accepted and persisted, second is handled without creating a second queue record.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is translation of queue backpressure signal into HTTP status/header contract. MOCK-MISS: needs module interaction (queue error/state -> HTTP mapping), not just one function if wiring can drop headers/status. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network required; queue adapter can deterministically report full. No LLM dependency. Rejection-gate for System: behavior text specifies protocol mapping, not real infrastructure timing/network faults.
behaviors.test_description: Configure queue adapter to return a 'full' condition on enqueue; assert handler responds 503 and includes valid `Retry-After` header.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This targets queue component behavior with persistence + capacity config interaction. Integration is minimum because it spans configuration, persistence layer, and queue admission logic. REAL-INFRA: local DB/filesystem-backed queue (in-process test env) is sufficient; no external network/subprocess required by behavior text. No LLM dependency. Rejection-gate for System: text says 'persistent queue' and 'configured capacity limit' but does not require external service/OS-only behavior beyond local persistence semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue backend with a small capacity, fill to limit, then attempt one more write and assert deterministic rejection without state corruption.
